concept.title: Profiling and Subject Scheme Validation
concept.shortdesc: DitaCraft validates profiling attribute values against subject scheme constraints, ensuring controlled vocabularies are respected.

section.title: Overview
section.p: Subject scheme maps define controlled vocabularies for profiling attributes such as audience, platform, product, and otherprops. When a subject scheme is present in your workspace, DitaCraft validates that profiling attribute values use only the allowed values defined by the scheme.

ol.li: DitaCraft automatically discovers subject scheme maps in your workspace (or from the explicit root map)
The Subject Scheme Service parses the scheme and extracts the controlled values for each attribute, building hierarchy paths for grouped values
When you use profiling attributes in your DITA content, their values are checked against the allowed values
Invalid values are reported with a diagnostic that lists the valid options
Auto-completion for profiling attributes shows only allowed values with hierarchy grouping (e.g., Platform > Linux > Ubuntu) and default value preselection

section.title: Subject Hierarchy Grouping
section.p: When subject scheme definitions use nested <subjectdef> elements to define a hierarchy, DitaCraft displays this hierarchy in auto-completion suggestions. For example:
When completing platform attribute values, you will see suggestions like:

ul.li: ubuntu — shown as os > linux > ubuntu
fedora — shown as os > linux > fedora

section.p: If the scheme defines a default value, that value is preselected in the completion list.

section.title: Validated Attributes
section.p: The following profiling attributes are validated when controlled by a subject scheme:
Attributes that are not constrained by a subject scheme are not validated — you can use any value freely.

section.title: Subject Scheme Example
section.p: A subject scheme map that constrains the platform attribute:
section.codeblock: <subjectScheme> <subjectdef keys="platform-values"> <subjectdef keys="windows"/> <subjectdef keys="linux"/> <subjectdef keys="macos"/> </subjectdef> <enumerationdef> <attributedef name="platform"/> <subjectdef keyref="platform-values"/> </enumerationdef> </subjectScheme>
section.p: With this scheme active, using platform="android" would produce a warning because android is not in the allowed values list.

strow.stentry: Attribute value not in controlled vocabulary. The diagnostic message lists the valid values.

section.title: Configuration
section.p: Subject scheme validation is enabled by default. To disable it:
